description: State-driven walkthrough guide. Each objective has start_conditions (when the guide may pick it) and completion_conditions (when critique marks it done).

steps.description: Exit Red's House 2F via the stairs to 1F
hints: Stairs are at the top-right of the room (approximately x=7, y=1)
Walk onto the S tile — it's a regular walkable tile. The warp fires automatically when you step on it.
After stepping on the stairs, wait a frame or two for the transition to fire.

steps.description: Exit Red's House 1F to Pallet Town via the south doormat
hints: Navigate to the S tiles (doormats) at the bottom of the room
Stand on the S tile, then keep walking south — the warp fires when you step off the tile in the exit direction.
The S tile is regular floor. The exit triggers by walking south off it, not by stepping onto it.

steps.description: Navigate to the north edge of Pallet Town to trigger the Oak encounter
hints: Navigate around the buildings to reach the north edge of the map.
The passage between buildings is at x=8 — go right from the entry point, then navigate north through the gap.
Grass tiles at y=1 (around x=10-11) trigger Oak's encounter.
Avoid the S tile at (5,5) which leads back to Red's House.

steps.description: Pick your first Pokemon! There are three Poke Balls on the table. Check each one to see what's inside, then choose the one you want.
hints: ★ THIS IS IT — your first Pokemon! The most exciting moment in the game!
There are 3 Poke Balls on the table. The GPS will guide you to each one.
At each ball: face toward it, press A, read the dialog to see what Pokemon is inside.
If it's NOT the one you want, press B to say NO. The dialog will close.
When you find the one YOU want, press A to say YES!

After saying YES, press B to advance through Oak's narration. Press B to skip nickname. If all balls have been checked move to the tile below the one that contains the starter Pokemon you want and press A.

steps.description: Defeat your rival's Pokemon in the first battle
hints: Walk south toward the exit door. The rival will block your path and challenge you.
Battle: use your starter's level 5 move (Tackle/Scratch/Vine Whip/Water Gun). Keep HP above 5.
After winning, Oak tells you to go to Viridian City Mart to pick up his parcel.

steps.description: Leave Oak's Lab and return to Pallet Town
hints: Walk south through the lab to the exit doormat at the bottom.
The exit is at the south wall — stand on the doormat tile, then continue walking south to trigger the warp.
You'll arrive in Pallet Town.

steps.description: Leave Pallet Town and travel north on Route 1 to Viridian City
hints: Head north through Pallet Town to Route 1, then continue north to Viridian City.
Route 1 has wild Pidgey and Rattata — they are weak, you can battle or avoid.

steps.description: Heal at the Viridian City Pokemon Center
hints: Step 1: Find the Pokemon Center in southwest Viridian City — look for a building with a warp tile near Route 1.
Step 2: Walk onto the warp tile inside the door to enter the Pokemon Center.
Step 3: Once INSIDE, walk north toward the counter. The nurse (NPC 0x29) is behind the counter.
Step 4: COUNTER NPC: Stand on the walkable tile directly below the counter (# above you), face up, press A to interact.
Step 5: Advance through her dialog (press A repeatedly) until your Pokemon are healed.
Step 6: After healing, walk south to the doormat and press down to exit.

steps.description: Get Oak's Parcel from the Viridian City Mart
hints: Step 1: Find the Viridian Mart in southeast Viridian City — look for a building with a warp/door tile.
Step 2: Enter the building and walk toward the counter at the back.
Step 3: COUNTER NPC: Stand on the walkable tile directly below the counter (# above you), face up, press A to interact.
Step 4: The clerk gives you Oak's Parcel.
Step 5: After this, has_oaks_parcel becomes true.

steps.description: Deliver Oak's Parcel to Professor Oak in Pallet Town
hints: Travel south from Viridian City through Route 1 to Pallet Town.
Go to Oak's Lab in Pallet Town.
Talk to Oak inside the lab (face him, press A).
He takes the parcel and gives you the Pokedex!
After delivery: Mart items unlock, Viridian north exit opens, trading enabled.

steps.description: Navigate through Viridian Forest to reach Pewter City
hints: Head north from Viridian City to Viridian Forest.
Forest has Bug-type Pokemon (Caterpie, Weedle) — good for grinding.
Navigate through the forest using collision map.
Exit north to Pewter City.
Train your Pokemon to at least level 10-12 before the gym.

steps.description: Defeat Brock at Pewter Gym to earn the Boulder Badge
hints: Pewter Gym is in the center of town.
Brock uses Rock-type Pokemon (Geodude Lv12, Onix Lv14).
Fire moves are NOT super effective against Rock in Gen 1 (bug).
Water moves (if you chose Squirtle) are super effective.
If you chose Charmander, use Scratch — it still works.
Growl to lower enemy Attack, then attack.
After winning, receive Boulder Badge and TM34 (Bide).

steps.description: Travel Route 3 to Mt. Moon
hints: Head east from Pewter City to Route 3.
Route 3 has stronger wild Pokemon — be prepared.
Train your Pokemon before entering Mt. Moon.

steps.description: Navigate through Mt. Moon
hints: Mt. Moon is a cave with Zubat, Geodude, and Clefairy.
Navigate through the cave using collision map.
Exit to Route 4 on the east side.

steps.description: Defeat Misty at Cerulean Gym to earn the Cascade Badge
hints: Cerulean City is north of Route 4.
Misty uses Water-type Pokemon (Staryu Lv18, Starmie Lv21).
Electric moves are super effective (if you caught a Pikachu).
Grass moves (Bulbasaur) are also super effective.
After winning, receive Cascade Badge and TM11 (Water Pulse).

steps.description: Defeat Lt. Surge at Vermilion Gym to earn the Thunder Badge
hints: Vermilion City is south of Cerulean via Route 6.
Lt. Surge uses Electric-type Pokemon (Voltorb, Pikachu, Raichu).
Ground moves are super effective but rare in Gen 1.
Use physical attacks — Raichu is fast and hits hard.

steps.description: Defeat Erika at Celadon Gym to earn the Rainbow Badge
hints: Celadon City is west of Vermilion via Route 7.
Erika uses Grass-type Pokemon (Victreebel, Tangela, Vileplume).
Fire moves are super effective (Charmander line).
Ice and Flying moves also work well.

steps.description: Defeat Koga at Fuchsia Gym to earn the Soul Badge
hints: Fuchsia City is south of Celadon via Route 18.
Koga uses Poison-type Pokemon (Koffing, Muk, Weezing).
Psychic and Ground moves are super effective.
Psychic types are dominant in Gen 1.

steps.description: Defeat Sabrina at Saffron Gym to earn the Marsh Badge
hints: Saffron City is between Celadon, Vermilion, and Lavender.
Sabrina uses Psychic-type Pokemon (Abra, Kadabra, Alakazam).
Bug and Ghost moves are theoretically super effective.
In Gen 1, Ghost moves bug against Psychic — use strong physical attacks.
This is one of the hardest gyms.

steps.description: Defeat Blaine at Cinnabar Gym to earn the Volcano Badge
hints: Cinnabar Island is south of Fuchsia via Route 19 (surf required).
Blaine uses Fire-type Pokemon (Growlithe, Ponyta, Rapidash).
Water and Rock moves are super effective.
If you chose Squirtle, this is straightforward.

steps.description: Defeat Giovanni at Viridian Gym to earn the Earth Badge
hints: Viridian Gym is in Viridian City (where you started).
Giovanni uses Ground-type Pokemon (Rhyhorn, Dugtrio, Nidoqueen, Nidoking, Rhydon).
Water and Grass moves are super effective.
This is the final gym badge.

steps.description: Defeat the Elite Four and become Champion
hints: Travel to Indigo Plateau (north of Victory Road).
Elite Four members: Lorelei (Ice), Bruno (Fighting), Agatha (Ghost), Lance (Dragon).
Champion is your rival (Blue/Gary) with a strong team.
Recommended team level: 55-65.
Stock up on Potions, Revives, and Full Restores before entering.
Psychic types are very strong in Gen 1 — consider having one.
